reviews.review_text:
There's a reason this game has influenced Dead Space and many other third person over the shoulder games that came after.

DO NOT ever skip this just because of the remake, some things the Remake did better, some of it this version does. (For both story, characterization and enemy encounters) Unlike the RE1 Remake where most would consider the remake to be the definitive version, I still recommend playing this first before the remake just to see what are the differences between the two.

The game comes packaged with Separate Ways, Assignment Ada (That the remake doesn't have) and Mercenaries. Do download RE4_tweaks on github to fix the mouse aiming as well for a smoother experience.

100%ing the achievements are pretty easy:
- Play the game entirely
- Complete all Merchant Gun range stages and collect all bottle caps
- Play Separate Ways to unlock the 2nd set of costumes for the all costumes achievement
- Do a 2nd fresh run on Professional difficulty (I consider this harder than the Remake personally)

[h2] The gameplay style, atmosphere, and replayability are incredible. Everything about this game is perfect. It’s surreal that a game this fun can exist. [/h2]

Optimization: you’ll need re4_tweaks to run the game without headaches  (hd project already come with it).

Got it on sale for 5$ and installed the Resident Evil 4 HD project + RE4 tweaks for free. Then I was blown away by how great a modded GameCube game looks and plays on PC! (even with keyboard & mouse) The game has a nice soundtrack and I also enjoyed the story & dialogue even though it's a bit campy. I'd highly recommend this game and those 2 mods, it's a lot of fun and a great deal for 5$. Kudos to Capcom for promoting the HD project by pinning it in RE4's steam discussions instead of trying to shut it down.
this game is pretty awesome ngl. imo, better than the remake. IN. MY. OPINION, let us share opinions on the internet :/

This is easily my favorite Resident Evil game out of all of them. Just an amazing blend of action, spookiness, and a dash of jank. You have the best character (Leon) to lead the charge and save the presidents daughter (who yells).
 
Games with this style of graphics where things are easy to see and without too much clutter, are perfect. It is just a very fun and smooth ride from start to finish. No need to worry about weapon durability, crafting, or any cumbersome mechanics.

Weapon variety is great for players with different styles and all weapons are useful. I used the Punisher pistol from the moment I got it until the end even though the damage isn't the greatest simply due to the fact it can pierce shields, thin cover, and more than one enemy.  

Tip: Make sure to buy the Rocket Launcher before every boss (unless doing a challenge run)

This is a fantastic game. The gameplay, atmosphere, locations are just amazing. Though I admit it took a while to get used to the controls (the game has tank controls basically).

If you get this game make sure to get re4_tweaks as it has many bugfixes and quality-of-life things like FOV increase or ability to reload without aiming.

This game is a masterpiece. I played it since I was seven years old, and back then, it was the best horror game experience I’ve ever had. I still play it occasionally, and then I stumbled upon the PC version. I’ve tried many mods that have given this game its old feel with new features, challenges, and adventures.I’ve tried numerous mods that have breathed new life into this game, restoring its classic charm while introducing fresh features, challenges, and adventures. I highly recommend it to anyone who’s eager to immerse themselves in the captivating story of this game. It remains playable and enjoyable even today.
Pretty fun for such an old game. Be sure to use the "re4_tweaks" mod to improve the gameplay so Leon turns with your mouse rather than you being forced to use the arrow keys.

This is easily my favorite from 2000 and PlayStation 2 era. It gave soo much Childhood memory when i was playing it, the vibe they gave when i was playing this as a kid just stuck at my head until i play it again. 

Resident Evil 4 is game out of all of them. Just an amazing blend of action, spookiness, and a dash of jank. You have the best character (Leon) to lead the charge and save the presidents daughter, Ashley (who like to yells).

Games with this style of graphics where things are easy to see and without too much clutter, are perfect. It is just a very fun and smooth ride from start to finish. No need to worry about weapon durability, crafting, or any cumbersome mechanics.

Weapon variety is great for players with different playstyles and all weapons are useful. I used the Red9 pistol from the moment I got it until the end even though the recoil and reload speed isn't the greatest, buy i love the critical head when you shoot the head of the enemy.

Tip: Make sure to buy the Rocket Launcher before every boss (unless doing a challenge run)

Man playing it now on a pc rather than a ps2 made me feel like a pro of this game a lot of bosses i fought in the ps2 felt harder bc of the controller but here they're easy and mora predictable than back then
Incredibly fun game. while I prefer the remake, this IS really good. and for a game THIS old, it's got some beautiful graphics.
I enjoyed this one more then the remake! Great game and I can see the hype around this game with its atmosphere, environment, and combat style. Overall 10/10!!!

[h1] Overrated [/h1]
[u] 8/10 [/u]
RE4 is an iconic game. The amount of new, fresh ideas and mechanics it introduced to gaming back when it first came out is nothing short of stunning. However, I can't help but feel like the community is glazing it too much. RE4 (and RDR2) are the two most over hyped games in the history of gaming. I have too many issues with it to consider it a must play masterpiece. I wouldn't even say RE4 is the best game from 2005, (God of War takes the crown for me) let alone of all time. There are many positive things I can say about it, sure, but there are many negative things I can say as well. For example, I was personally never a fan of the Village / Castle / Island areas. Always felt boring and unappealing to me. Gameplay could get very repetitive too. True, they constantly introduce new enemies, weapons and utility to make the experience feel constantly fresh, but at the end of the day you're still just walking forward and shooting everything that moves. There are very few puzzles in the game, which is something I personally really enjoy in RE games. RE4 is also the first mainline RE game which slowly steps away from horror, and focuses more on action, something I've personally always considered a mistake for the franchise. My biggest complaint however is centered around performance issues. I often dropped down to 5-10 FPS in some areas of the game for no real reason. Even the Remake ran better at max settings. Speaking of the Remake, it changes too much from the original, therefore I would recommend playing both versions, as they are fundamentally different games and offer very different experiences.

wanted to replay this on steam after requiem, and it's still so much fun. compared to re4 remake, it feels like a very fast-paced strategic chess game; being forced to open your inventory to switch weapons mid-combat and getting that quick breather is great. it's incredibly satisfying when you get that pacing right, and it works perfectly with the restricted movement while aiming.
This is an amazing game even though it is old. The controls are a little tank like but its fine once you get used to it. Love the game and cant wait to play the remake.
This is one of the best games i have played 10/10 (REMAKE TOO) Apart from the controls there is no issue  about OG RE4.

One of the best games i've ever played, believe me, you wont regret buying this masterpiece, and now in HD!?
10/10
Absolute classic, gameplay holds up.

I love this game.
And yes, this port is kinda lazy : they promised a “visual overhaul” and “upgraded textures”, in reality we have couple of small graphical bugs (not present in the original), one cutscene (during the Castle) that they forgot to redo, they also didn’t work on the cutscenes from “Separate Way”, they’re still some low res enemies models in some custcenes (like when Leon and Ashley run towards the bridge)… There is a reason why fans created the “HD Project”.

And yet, despite this, I still recommend this port because it’s basically the Wii version without the motion controls, that looks better and has the extra content.

Also, Resident Evil 4 is simply one of the best games ever made. 

The atmosphere, the art direction, the level design, the gameplay, the amazing mercenaries mode, everything works wonderfully. There is a reason why so many (including his creators) have tried many times to recreate this timeless classic and never managed to do it.

And yes, while the remake was fun, this original is much better.
Hard to compare it to the remake, but absolutely amazing itself. Would highly recommend the HD Project mod, makes the game look and run so much better.

i absolutely ADORE this game, it was the first resident evil game i finished and it single handedly got me DEEEEP into the franchise. it has so many hours worth of content packed into one game. despite being over 20 years old this game really does hold up, though the controls are indeed pretty janky but they arent that hard to get used to. the shooting mechanics are smooth, guaranteeing  some very clean shots. the entire atmosphere of re4 is a complete vibe. the dialogue in this game is admittedly cheesy but honestly it really adds to that charm this game has.
No doubt has better graphics and everything but og just hit's different loved it plus it's cheaper
i replay the hell out of this game, man the gameplay loop is awesome, and dont forget to install mods, i would love to play resident evil 4 for the first time again.

Still got it, over 20 years later. Often imitated but never duplicated. The incredible limb-shooting, roundhouse kicking, weapon-upgrading adventure with spooky zombies and other nefarious creatures. 

RE4 Tweaks in particular breathes new life into what would otherwise be a dubious mouse and keyboard experience. The RE4 HD project which includes it is also worth checking out.

It may be because it was my first, but to me this is the best entry in the entire Resident Evil series and easily one of the top three games I've ever played. Never have I ever replayed something as many times as RE4. 5/5
This game is so deserving of the praise it gets, impossible to put down the second you start it.

This game is perfect — in every single sense of the word. The characters and atmosphere are exceptionally well designed, and the world pulls you in from the very first moment. Among its many highlights is one of the most iconic appearances of Leon S. Kennedy in the entire franchise, which is fitting given that Resident Evil 4 is one of the best-selling entries in the series.

While the story leans more toward action and spectacle than pure horror, that never feels like a flaw. The game achieves exactly what it sets out to do: entertain and deliver an unforgettable experience from start to finish.

Resident Evil 4 (2005) is a good game, but no more than that.
If you played one resident evil game then you can pretty much guess what this game is like.
This is a pretty old game, but holds up nowadays pretty well as well.
The gameplay is ok, the stroy is ok, the characters are ok.
The biggest CON for this game in my opinion is the movement and the dodge system.
Movement is pretty akward, because you turn only with your full body, looking up and down without aiming is practically unusable.
As for the dodging using keyboard and mouse the dodge prompt is either left mouse button+right mouse button, or x+c. Left+right mouse button is alright, but x+c was a horrbile choice, because they are a combination that not so easy to press in a hurry. So taking a hit because of that happen a lot. Also the prompt maybe on the screen to dodge and you press it in time however if the character does not have space to dodge you get hit regardless, so dodging does not give invicibilty frames in my experience.
I did not find most of the boss fights good, because the player characters mobility is horrible while most of the bosses are nimble.

Is it worth buying this game if you own the remake? I think it is worth it. The remake is pretty faithfull to this game, but there are enough differences between the two to buy this game if you like RE4 or just RE generaly. Also this one has all the game mods while the remake do not, or at least not for free.

It is wise to wait for a sale, because of the age of the game.

Having played the remake first before the classic, I thought my expectations would be low becasue of the controls but I did get used to it and eventually having fun and enjoyed it thoroughly as equally as the remake. And in it's own way, its charming, campy, aloof premise and the fact it doesn't take itself seriously at most part adds to the enjoyment and random wacky segments in the game which is bizarre. Glad I did try the classic a chance. And it's a good entryway to the RE franchise as well!

A timeless action horror game that never needed a remake. 8.5/10 

Where do I even start? after the short first few cutscenes the game just starts and never puts on the brakes. Sure, the gameplay is pretty simple and could even get repetitive if not for the amazing pacing and amount of just....STUFF this game has. Whenever you think this game is going even a little stale, it has something up its sleeve to surprise you, and this feeling and pacing goes on for the entire game.  

The gameplay, while simple, is also secretly pretty robust. where you aim and shoot at matters, A LOT.
 Every weapon might seem like they serve a simple purpose and some might even feel like they are only used in one specific scenarios, this is the feeling I had with Flash Grenades. But I randomly found out that they can instantly kill an enemy, if their head wields a tentacle. and I thought that was pretty cool. I dont know if other Resident Evil games do this kind of stuff since this is my first one!

Story is...okay? The characters are over the top but they arent exactly deep or rememberable, at least in this game. Sure Leon is a badass and Ada makes my ding-a-ling twitch ill give em that. The story though its just really some dumb stuff to make you have at least some motivation and reason why you are shooting up an entire village. The villain just has the basic ''Evil because I'm evil'' plan and ideology. But I'm certain story was never the main attraction of this this game.

Resident Evil 4 also has excellent atmosphere that you will not find anywhere else.

The only bad stuff that I can think of in this game are pretty much universal in every game that came out as console exclusives in the 2000s. FOV is small and was meant for bigger TVs and not PC monitors. Sure, someone could make the argument that it helps with the intensity and horror for the game, but it really just made me more nauseous. EVERY INTERACTION was also put on one button only, which sometimes made me screw up in what I was trying to do in specific situations. The QTE's this game has were more funny than annoying in my opinion, but sometimes you would only have a fraction of a second to react, and having them in cutscenes also were pretty annoying. especially when I tend to just put my controller on the table and enjoy the cinematic...however if you fail you can just skip to the QTE instantly, but idk if thats actually in the game or the mod I installed (re4_tweaks) does that.

Absolutely a must play for EVERYONE that plays games. However if you dont like responsive and original gameplay, or you like lackluster atmosphere, or you like bad art direction, you can always play the remake!

It's a timeless classic. I remember my first experience with this game was seeing it being played in a dingy asian restaraunt on a tv, on xbox 360. As a kid, I saw the briefcase inventory full of weapons including a giant rocket launcher and I thought to myself "That's sick." 
Now having played through this game myself, I can confirm, its sick. Finding treasure, upgrading weapons and fighting new bosses and monsters is just such a classic game loop, and the cherry on top is the B-movie esc dialogue that ensures the game isn't too serious and keeps it fun. A great game I would recommend to anyone.

game crashed on the last chapter before the last boss

other than that i rec playing this on M&K for aiming etc

Excellent game that still holds up today as long as you install the re4_tweaks mod. It also differs more enough to warrant a playthrough even if you've played the remake. The villains are far better in this version of the game, though the rest of the characters are better in the remake.

The game does feel a bit unbalanced at times, but thanks to frequent checkpoints no section feels insurmountable - only irritating at worst. And the quality does feel lower from the Island section onwards, but it's still fun and engaging.

JUST BUY IT, great game and worth playing in the modern day even just for the gaming history aspect. For a game from 2005 (over 20 years ago now) it still holds up insanely well. I had nostalgia for this from the remakes and playing the PS2 version as a teen. There is just this special formula to the clunky purposeful controls and enemies casually strolling at you that somehow nails the tension.

There are of course some issues: A lot of areas feel cramped or modeled too small, the items in the world scale strangely to character size being huge, the controls are dated and can make it difficult to finely or quickly position Leon, and the graphics are simultaneously better and worse than you'd think.

However, the parts they got right they absolutely nailed in a way that is not often recreated.
Udah gitu langsung keitung buat task Community Ambassador 👍 :v

Overall Verdict: Resident Evil 4 is a genre-defining masterpiece that takes the franchise in a bold, unique direction. While it has a few narrative hiccups and anti-climactic moments near the end, it remains an absolute blast to play.

THE GOOD:

    Action-Horror Pioneer: I don't agree with the game being marketed as traditional "survival horror" since enemies literally drop ammo as rewards. It is definitely action horror, but the survival tension is still there—it's just found in how you manage your money!

    The Merchant & Replayability: The Merchant is a brilliant system. Having to choose between upgrading your weapons or expanding your inventory box adds great strategy. Combined with health upgrades and Professional mode, the game heavily encourages multiple playthroughs.

    Stellar Environments: The graphics are genuinely incredible for 2005. Furthermore, the Village and Castle sections are absolutely stellar. I was worried the Castle was just going to repeat Code: Veronica, but it completely stands on its own.

    Sassy Leon: Leon is fantastic here. He spends so long being controlled by the Plaga, so when Saddler reaches his hand out and Leon just acts smug and talks back to him, I actually cheered.

    Great Characters & Antagonists: Luis Sera is a great supporting character with a genuinely tragic backstory. On the villain side, Salazar, Krauser, and Saddler are some of my favorite antagonists in the series.

    Thrilling Encounters: Salazar and Krauser have fantastic boss fights. Also, Salazar's "pets" in the sewers are incredibly tough and gave me classic Resident Evil 1 Hunter vibes.

THE BAD:

    Claustrophobic Camera: The camera sits just a bit too close to Leon for my liking.

    Underutilized Mendez: Bitores Mendez doesn't feel like an active, threatening presence while you're in the village. He doesn't get much to do, though his actual boss fight is still good.

    Saddler's Anti-Climax: Saddler is a great villain, but his boss fight is really bad. It’s way too easy and relies on glaringly obvious weak spots.

THE LORE / SEPARATE WAYS:

    Poor Krauser: He has an absolutely epic boss fight, but he has zero real connection to Leon other than "I died in a crash." He sort of just falls over and dies... until you play Separate Ways. He somehow survives Leon, only for Ada to anti-climactically kill him off.

    Separate Ways Feels Off: The DLC campaigns are okay, but Ada often feels awkwardly shoved into places she really shouldn't be.

    Clueless on Wesker: Wesker is just awkwardly looming in the background the entire time. It really makes me think Capcom had absolutely no clue what to do with him in this installment.

Conclusion: A massive step forward for the franchise with incredible pacing and replayability, even if the lore and a few late-game encounters stumble.
Peak main story, separate ways was alright, it kept reusing cutscenes from the basegame, and ada assignment was straight cheeks. like i hate fighting krauser.

Puta que pariu.

É tudo de bom, mas os puzzles são bem fáceis, e, apesar da câmera ter sido revolucionária, é chato de se movimentar com ela. Direto acontece um bug onde você toma grab e a câmera fica se mexendo, te impedindo de mirar.

Queria que o Leon do remake fosse igualzinho esse. Foda-se que após passar por um trauma absurdo você não conseguiria enfrentar tudo do re4 com sarcasmo e 0 preocupações🙄🙄.

Resident Evil 4 is a great experience, once you get the mods installed to fix all the whacky whoohoo port nosense. This game is a fine supermarket cheese, stinky, creamy, and has hot men sprinkled inside. Follow Leon Bingo Kennedy while he tries to rescue the presidents daughter, and save the world as a side activity. RE4 genuinely is a fun experience from the characters, to the writing, there's a reason this game got meme'd to hell and back, cause it's actually really good.

I think the highlights of this game is the tone and vibes. Every character feels purposeful, no moment is wasted with dumb crap (For the most part), and the jokes... Oh momma they are so bad but so good. Pacing wise, I never got bored or wanted it to end for the most part (We'll come back to this). The gameplay is pretty good too, where the guns feel amazing to use, sure, some aren't as good as others, but you very much fall into using your daily drivers soon and the game never punishes you for doing so. All these alone, makes it a solid 8/10 game,

BUT

It's not all sunshine's and buckfast sadly, this port can be rather difficult and annoying to work with if you just cold boot it without any mods. Plus, to me, RE4 doesn't know if it wants to be it's own thing or something new when it comes to gameplay. The newest gameplay thing is that we are now over the shoulder, and not using fixed camera's like the previous 4 games used. And as I said, for the most part it works, where it doesn't work is that it's still a tank control based game, you can only walk and turn, no strafing allowed. Which is ok for the opening like 4 hours, but then you hit the castle section, and you really wish you had more degree's of movement. Also no moving while aiming, you have to stand in place and shoot. This feels like they just took RE 1-3 movement system and pasted here, when really they should of done what they did with 5 and 6.

Also the final section kind of drags, the island is good, but it feels like it overstays it's welcome with too many elaborate set pieces and no actually meat to play with, just how Leon likes it.

The remake fixed a bunch of these issues for me, though the island still stinks in remake, however the remake loses the B movie charm I adored in this and that makes me sad...

So all in all, Local Evil Residents Biohazard 4 is really good, but it let down by the virtue of it coming out in 2005 and having Bagade of the old RE games movement system. But it good, I recommend, get it a soon as. 

Grins (:

I love you daddy Leon. The controls are terrible though and I'd Highly recommend buying the remake. Game is quite difficult too but older games and all Resident Evils games are regardless, except maybe the more action focused 5 or 6.

My biggest issue with the classic "Resident Evil" games has always been the tank controls and fixed camera angles, which sometimes made it difficult for me to feel fully immersed in the experience. That’s why "Resident Evil 4" felt like such a huge step forward for the series. It took the original formula and evolved it in a way that made the gameplay feel much more dynamic and engaging. There’s no surprise that so many people consider it one of the greatest Resident Evil games ever made.

That being said, some of the older design choices are still present. The stop-and-shoot combat system took a bit of getting used to, and at times it felt clunky compared to modern standards. Having already played the remake first, going back to the original was definitely a strange experience. It’s impossible not to notice how much gaming has evolved since then.

However, when you remember when this game originally released, it becomes incredibly impressive. "Resident Evil 4" was truly ahead of its time, and the reason the remake turned out so well is because the original source material was already that strong. The remake may have modernized the gameplay, but the foundation was built by a game that completely changed the direction of the franchise and influenced many titles that came after it.

Even if I personally prefer the remake, I can absolutely see why the original is considered such an iconic game and why it remains so beloved by fans to this day.

And one final thing...
Ashley, please... can't you just stay quiet for five minutes?!😭

The game is awesome. However there are many quick time events that randomly change when you’re trying to play. 
In particular I’ve been stuck on a bugged quick time event which happens at the end of a very long sequence with no saves 
Can’t they just fix qtes to work consistently? The game has been out for like 15 years now
Still one of the best games of all time and geinunely fun, defi worth it to play it or revisit it

I've played this game on WII back in the day, F-ing Excellent!. I played the steam version with the HD Mod, F-ing Excellent again! One of my favorite games of all time. I recently replayed it 5 times in a row and enjoyed it every single time. This game deserves a F-ing ten out of ten!

I will start off this review by saying that if you have any doubts about this game purely because it's from an older generation and may not hold up to games nowadays, I will gladly say that this game holds up fantastically well and is genuinely still on par with games from nowadays. The controls and feel of the game may take some getting used to, but once you master then, you barely even notice that you're playing a game from 2005, though i will recommend installing the RE4Tweaks mod to customize the controls and game to your liking (I only really used it to make running a toggle instead of a hold). I had so much fun playing this game and quite literally cannot think of any gripes or complaints I had with this one, definitely a must-play if you are interested in the Resident Evil franchise.

I've really liked the game so far being prolly on of the if not best game in the franchise I recommend playing the whole series. The main downside is how hard it is to play for me as a casual player being not really good at shooter games otherwise nothing i dislike about it.
Resident Evil 4 is my personal favorite RE game. You should buy it, it's amazing

This was my first RE game i beat and after playing them in order this amazes me how it keeps the formula but also has a ton of changes and i actually think the changes work because under it all you can still tell it is Resident Evil in its bones

Something i also really like about it is how there are scenes i audibly laughed at because how funny they were but there were gameplay momments where i was scared and things were tense

Since this is one of my favorite games of all time this is a easy 10/10 of a game

Truly legendary staple of the action horror genre. Memorable characters, decent story, VERY addicting core gameplay loop, fun unlockables, cool arsenal, very good side content and the mercenaries mode is very replayable. it strayed from its survival horror roots in the first 3 games but man it's an amazing time from start to finish. This version also supports 4K

Played it when I was a kid.
Playing it again when I am an adult now.
The amount of horror is still the same. Best game.
damn this game is so long i had forgotten how long exactly but its a really good game. :)

Definitely more of an action game than horror, but it sure is fun and still holds up 20 years later. The one gripe I have is its linearity combined with its length, which can make it a bit of a drag to play through multiple times.

l really enjoy the game l have played it on the PS2 back in the day and it was pretty fun kicking the villagers and other shenanigans and ashley l know some of you would say it was pain in the butt and all but we got used to it l think that's all

"Story of my Life"

Bought this during the Spring Sale as my first ever Resident Evil game, and at first… I honestly didn’t get the hype 😭
The first hour felt kinda slow and I almost dropped it, but because of the game’s reputation I decided to keep playing.

Best decision ever.

After a few more chapters I got completely addicted and somehow ended up putting 18+ hours into it in just 4 days 
I still haven’t even finished the story yet, but this game already has some of the best atmosphere and pacing I’ve experienced in a while.

Also whoever created the Regenerators needs psychological evaluation because those things are genuinely terrifying and weird as hell 😭

This game basically turned me into a Resident Evil fan overnight, and I’m definitely trying the other RE games after this.

Resident Evil 4 is an 11/10 and still the best Resident Evil, I don’t care.

Yeah yeah, the old ones were great too, respect to them, but people acting like tank controls are some sacred untouchable thing need to relax. Cry harder, pus**ies, this game clears them.

Everything here just hits perfectly. The creepy rural Spain vibe, the villagers screaming nonsense while chasing you with farming tools, the sound design, the weapons feeling powerful as hell after some cool upgrades, the inventory tetris addiction, the ridiculous one-liners… it’s nonstop quality.

And the replayability is insane. You finish it once and instantly wanna go again with better guns, more money, different unlocks. The game practically feeds your addiction itself, it's lowkey one of the best games to speedrun or chase achievements.

Leon is cool as hell, the merchant is legendary, enemies are memorable, bosses are insane, this thing just does not miss.

11/10, gaming perfection with a shotgun.
quickly became one of my fav re games. so fun and interactive. perfect mix of horror and action.

The myth, the legend, the one revolutionary Resident Evil that brought fame and glory to the franchise. Its extra content (one minigame, one side story, and one massive game mode), offers VERY high replayability. Despite being two decades old, it has aged better than the finest wines. Its graphics still deliver an AWESOME atmospheric experience, and some of its music ranks among the best in the franchise. The safe room music used to put me to sleep. In fact, it was my personal treatment for insomnia! Sometimes I lose track of time playing Resident Evil 4. I get completely carried away, fighting the temptation to keep going to the point of hearing myself screaming in my head: "LEEEEOOOONNNN, HEEELLLPPP!" 😭

As someone who has a lot of love for resident evil, I can really vouch for Re4 2005 as its mechanics in my opinion still hold up as despite tank controls being considered dated, this game was intricately designed with such mechanics in mind as once I got used to it, controlling Leon felt as smooth as butter and super responsive, also the art aesthetics of this game are beautiful, the character designs really cool and the story is very entertaining with a lot of campy humour, while still containing interesting thematics to back up the story making the game more meaningful and the horror aspects more unsettling, it’s worth a single play through to unlock mercenaries.

this game is honestly the game that changed my life, i was lacking confidence and things in life that made me somewhat sad and this game boosted me up in having a better lifestyle and a better mood and patience this is actually a masterpeice and should be a must buy.
install mods to fix stuff like autolose QTEs but genuinely phenomenal game holds up incredibly well
i dont really like the camera stuff butttt its fun i really like it

Playing this a decade after religiously playing it every summer since launch and it is still a fantastic game. The atmosphere, especially in the village is hard to match and few games still manage to make me dread an upcoming section/water room. Solid gameplay, great music and a decent story. That being said, there are some awful sections, and the game starts to drag ass when nearing the end of the castle. The island made me pause and contemplate whether a third round was worth it. On that particular day, it was not and I imagine that this will be the case until I decide to replay this in a decade or so.

It's a good game! in my opiniion the remake is slightly better (for new players and in general) but the og one is good too! it may take you a while to learn the movement of the game but it's still a very very very good game! + don't shhot the merchent...

This is my favorite game, full-stop. I fist played it in 2005 on GameCube and decided to replay it this year. I was amazed at how well it holds up. The controls, which I remember being hard to get used to, we're not as bad as I remembered and I quickly got into the flow of combat. 

This go round I noticed the heavy lifting the sound design was doing in terms of scares. The quick time events weren't as bad as I remembered, though it was annoying having to be "on edge" during cutscenes. 

The cheesy story was like a warm blanket, though I get why some people might cringe at some of the dialogue. For me, the gameplay loop, loot collecting and weapon upgrading are the best of the best and what made me come back for a second round.

10/10 will play again, Stranger

I PLAYED THE REMAKE FIRST! I LIKE THE REMAKE MORE! You don't miss anything playing the Remake without the Original, and vice versa. The differences are microscopic to small at most, that being said it wasn't redundant playing the same game again, I had fun twice. I understand the hype for the Original, the mechanics are timeless for a 2005 title, worthy of the Triple A title. I just don't understand why the Remake gets some flak, they're both great. Pick up either, but if you pick up both you wont regret it, especially on sale.

this is the start of "Modern" Resident Evil and with it the fundamentals of modern 3rd person shooters. its's got good art direction. it's excels at conveying so many different horror vibes from the midnight graveyard to the mad scientist lab. the shooting mechanics themselves are very satisfying and the way scarcity works will have you gritting your teeth with every shot. the story is enjoyably cheesy, when it isn't being painfully sexist regarding ashley, the damsel. the monsters have creepy design that will haunt your nightmares. Leon is funny (and sexy if if your into that kind of thing). totally recommend. play the game with the RE4 HD project mod. (re4hd,com) the guys who made it went on a hunt for the sources for most of the original textures and their work pays off, especially if you look closely.

From RE 0 to 4, worst RE I've played so far. The controls feel worse than any classic RE I've played, the voice acting is cringe (and very, very racist) and there's no horror / puzzle-solving experience at all. I'll have to give the remake a try.

Whoever says this is the best Resident Evil is just nostalgic for their teenage years. The controls are clunky even by 2005 standards. You cannot move and shoot or slash which is necessary in a survivor shooter that requires maneuverability. The PC version has the added problem of running way higher frame  rates than the original console so you have half the time to react to quick time events. Some of the bosses on Professional difficulty just spam these events because again the cooldown is based on frame rates. Buy the remake, the original is trash.

Resident Evil 4 — A Legendary Game Held Back by Its Controls

I love the Resident Evil series for its story, characters, and overall lore, but honestly, playing the original Resident Evil 4 today feels very difficult. Just like Resident Evil 5, the controls are extremely outdated and uncomfortable. At times, it feels almost impossible to play smoothly by modern standards.

I understand why the game is considered legendary — it influenced an entire generation of action games and survival horror titles. The atmosphere, pacing, and iconic moments are still great. But the gameplay itself hasn’t aged well for me.

That’s why I really hope the remake handles things better. From what I’ve seen, the modernized controls and improved movement make the experience much more enjoyable while still preserving the spirit of the original.

I’ll always respect Resident Evil 4 for its impact on gaming, but personally, the original controls make it hard to fully enjoy today.

After finishing the game, with a heavy heart I'm changing the review to negative because there is absolutely no reason to play this instead of the remake. If there was no remake, rating it today with the forgiving optics for a more than 20-year-old game, it would deserve 6, maybe even 7 out of 10 if I'm stretching it. But honestly, I'm glad it's over and have no wish to replay it or try the Separate Ways. Actually, I wish I just invested the time into replaying the Remake again.

Remake is a masterpiece and one of the best games ever with incredible replay value. The 2005 original didn't age well, and on PC it's unplayable with the horrible tank controls (those were outdated even in 2005). The 2014 remaster at least adds mouse control, even though even the WASD style is still outdated and annoying. 
QTEs are generally nonsensically irritating or very buggy to the point that people are using other bugs in an attempt to bypass them.
Ashley has barely any character, and all the female characters are written in a pretty sexist way; dialogues feel cheesy, especially almost anything Leon says.
There's no real atmosphere, no fear or anything. It still is RE4, but it's more like an action game with super simple puzzles.
On the highest difficulty on the first playthrough, it's pretty easy, and the most immense challenges are annoying controls, QTEs, and inventory overflowing with all possible ammo and healing items.

I don't think this even aged well in comparison to games from that period. You can find many 90s games that are just as awesome today as they were back then.
It's not a bad game, but it truly made me appreciate the Remake.

I think people are just adding some nostalgia when they rate OG RE4. I didn't play it back then, so I'm rating it in present times with experience with everything else.

This is something to play for sentimental or historical reasons, but if you're deciding between Remake and this, there is really nothing to think about.
Its graphics was not bad as other people talked about

RE4 is a classic for a reason. It's certainly a little dated, but not in a bad way. Some of it's design quirks can be pretty frustrating, but never so much so that it ruins the fun too much. The characters are iconic, and the story is actually pretty solid all things considered. I think my only big complaint is the pacing gets pretty poor toward the end, when it heavily steers away from horror and puzzles for strait action and almost every room you enter just turns into a shooting gallery forcing you to quickly burn through all of the ammo you've saved up. From time to time there are some pretty poorly designed rooms that trap you into extremely tight spaces with a few too many enemies, but I guess that's part of the fun/stress of it all. It also has some pretty poor messaging from time to time, where you're quickly supposed to react to a situation, but you have basically no idea what you're supposed to do and it will lead to you dying just to learn what you need to do.

Overall, it's a really solid game, even after it's turned 20, and it's certainly worth a play through if you enjoy older titles, linear action games, or survival horror. I don't think it's quite as good as the legends say, but that doesn't mean it's not a great game.
This game holds a special place in my heart. Loved playing this in the mid-2000's. Totally worth getting
